title: Audit whether current documentation is still valid
description:
User wants a backlog task that evaluates whether the project's CURRENT documentation is still valid/accurate — i.e. a documentation-audit task. User explicitly asked for it to be a **needs-human** (gated) task, so on conversion it must be authored gate:"needs-human" with NO facets (per the harness's needs-human carve-out).

Intent: verify the docs still reflect reality rather than having drifted. This project treats docs as part of 'done' (root CLAUDE.md golden rule 3: docs update in lockstep with code, same commit; README.md is the declared source of truth for *what is currently implemented*). Over an autonomous loop run that lockstep can silently slip, so a periodic human validation pass is the intent here.

Candidate docs in scope (for conversion to decide/narrow — NOT resolving here): README.md (implementation-status section — the source of truth for current state), root CLAUDE.md (conventions), .harness/custom/docs/LIMITATIONS.md (the project trade-off/limitations log — rows there carry 'revisit when…' triggers that may now be due, e.g. StoreKit stub → T062, content fixture → real scenario packs), design docs under .harness/docs/designs/ (e.g. design-spec.md, architecture.md), and any PLAN.md. Note: anything whose scope touches .harness/** is REQUIRED to be needs-human anyway (per .harness/CLAUDE.md), which reinforces the user's needs-human choice.

Why needs-human fits well beyond the user's request: judging whether prose documentation is 'valid' is an inherent judgment call (accuracy, staleness, whether a 'revisit' trigger has fired) that a cold unattended builder can't reliably make — it's exactly the kind of review a human should eyeball.

Deferred to conversion (do NOT decide now): exact doc set, what 'valid' means as an acceptance bar, whether it's one audit task or split per-doc, and whether it produces follow-up fix tasks for whatever it finds stale.
